relatedness_reasoning: Both files involve the same customer and originate from fraud/risk detection workflow; risk_and_compliance receives fraud reports and initiates KYC refresh requests as a compliance response, forming a logical pipeline step.

mappings.reasoning: Both are unique identifiers for the request/report; reportId is repurposed as the KYC refresh request identifier.
reportDate marks when the fraud report was created; this timestamp likely triggers the KYC refresh request creation.
targetDeployment is 'risk_and_compliance', which matches the department_from enum value.
No inbound field specifies the destination department 'credit'; this is a system-determined routing decision.
Inbound severity 'high' maps to outbound priority 'HIGH'; fraud severity drives KYC refresh urgency.
Exact semantic match with naming convention change from camelCase to snake_case.
customerName directly maps to full_name with naming convention change.
No date of birth field exists in the inbound fraud report schema.
No national ID or passport field exists in the inbound schema; only account number is provided.
No email field exists in the inbound fraud report schema.
No phone field exists in the inbound fraud report schema.
No historical KYC refresh date exists in the inbound fraud report.
Fraud report triggers KYC refresh, though reportType 'scam_fraud' does not directly map to 'PERIODIC_REVIEW' enum value.
escalationRequired boolean indicates risk flagging; fraud incidents requiring escalation trigger risk flags.
Inbound severity 'high' maps to outbound risk_level 'MEDIUM' or higher; severity indicates risk assessment.
riskIndicators array is transformed into trigger_factors array; individual indicator codes become trigger factors.
Fraud/scam incident types are transformed into compliance issue objects with issue_type, status, and severity.
No document requirements are specified in the inbound fraud report schema.
actionsTaken list describes mitigations; required_actions may derive from or relate to these actions, though semantics differ.
No deadline or completion date is specified in the inbound fraud report.
transactionStatus 'blocked' indicates incident handling; outbound status 'PENDING_CUSTOMER_RESPONSE' reflects KYC workflow state rather than transaction state.
additionalNotes free-text field maps to internal_notes with naming convention change.
